description_long: This is a vintage corded telephone with a rectangular, slightly sloped base and a classic rotary dial interface. The handset rests securely on top of the base, connected by a coiled cord that extends from the left side. The rotary dial is circular and features finger holes for dialing, with a central label area. The phone is primarily a light beige color, with subtle off-white and tan accents, and a graffiti-style marking on the rear side. The surface appears to be made of hard plastic, with a matte to slightly smooth finish. This object is typical of mid-to-late 20th-century telephones, often found in homes or private offices.
description: A vintage beige corded telephone with a rotary dial, coiled cord, and graffiti on the back. The handset rests on a sloped rectangular base.
description_view_0: Side view showing the handset resting on the base and the coiled cord extending outward.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rotary dial and the symmetrical placement of the handset.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a clear look at the handset and the rotary dial's profile.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the graffiti-style marking and the coiled cord trailing behind.
